cooperates.cooperate_desc: Tigreal pairs perfectly with Pharsa because his crowd control groups enemies together and protects the backline, allowing Pharsa to safely deal massive burst damage with her ultimate during team fights.
Atlas synergizes well with Pharsa because his powerful crowd control can lock multiple enemies in place, allowing her to safely unleash massive AoE damage from the backline during team fights.
Mathilda pairs well with Pharsa by providing mobility, shields, and crowd control, allowing Pharsa to reposition safely and deal consistent burst damage from a secure distance.

Ruby serves as a premier frontline guardian for Pharsa, using her relentless crowd control to create a safe zone for Pharsa's ultimate. By chaining her stuns and pulls, Ruby effectively anchors targets in place while simultaneously peeling off divers, ensuring Pharsa can channel her massive area-of-effect damage without interruption.

counters.counter_desc: Zetian ultimate can counter Pharsa during her ultimate, thus, canceling it.
Lancelot high mobility can make it hard for Pharsa to find the right position. Lancelot can dive in into her ultimate, thus, forcing Pharsa to cancel it.
Like Lancelot, Fanny also can dive into her ultimate (especialy when she has cable). Fanny is hard to catch with Pharsa first skill, making her hard to find a position to burst down her damage.

Akai's ultimate will force Pharsa to cancel hers. Akai's ultimate also can cancel when Pharsa in her bird's form (wings by wings), make it her hard to escape. Akai have high mobility that will forcing her to use her battle spells.

dominants.dominant_desc:
1. Focus on clearing minion waves quickly and poking the enemy hero.

2. Maintain good positioning to avoid ganks.

3. Rotate to nearby lanes when the enemy is low HP.

4. In team fights, stay behind the front line and deal damage safely.

emblems.emblem_desc: This emblem is chosen because Pharsa excels at long-range burst damage, and Lethal Ignition perfectly complements her playstyle. Her skills and ultimate can easily deal more than 7% of an enemy’s max HP multiple times in a short duration, activating the talent for extra adaptive damage. This makes her poke deadlier, helps secure kills faster during team fights, and increases her impact without needing to get close to enemies, keeping her safer while dealing high damage.

equips.equip_desc: This equipment build is chosen to maximize Pharsa sustained magic damage, survivability, and team fight impact. Arcane Boots provide magic penetration for stronger early damage, while Glowing Wand applies burn damage that synergizes well with her multi-hit ultimate. Lightning Truncheon boosts burst damage during skill casts, and Divine Glaive helps break through enemies with high magic defense. Holy Crystal greatly increases her magic power for late-game scaling, while Blood Wings grants extra survivability with a large HP shield, allowing Pharsa to deal consistent damage safely from long range.

The CD Reduction build is chosen to let Pharsa cast her skills and ultimate more frequently, increasing her pressure in team fights. Magic Boots and Genius Wand reduce cooldowns and enemy magic defense, allowing faster skill rotations and stronger damage over time. Lightning Truncheon and Holy Crystal boost burst and scaling damage, while Blood Wings adds survivability so Pharsa can stay longer in fights and continuously harass enemies from a safe distance.

hero.hero_overview: Pharsa is a mage that is known for her long-range burst damage and map-wide zoning ability. With her ultimate Feathered Air Strike, she can rain damage from a safe distance, making her deadly in team fights and objective control. Her passive Wings by Wings lets her transform into a bird, giving her insane mobility for rotations and escapes.
hero.hero_strength:
Very long attack range: can hit enemies without being close to danger

High burst damage: especially deadly in mid to late game

Strong team fight control: ultimate can zone, poke, or finish enemies

Fast rotation & escape: bird form allows quick roaming and repositioning

Good wave clear: clears minions fast, helping pressure lanes
hero.hero_weakness:
Very squishy (low HP & defense): easy to kill if caught

Highly dependent on positioning: one wrong spot = instant death

Ultimate can be interrupted: vulnerable to CC like stun or knock-up

Weak against assassins: heroes like Ling, Hayabusa, or Lancelot counter her hard

Needs good map awareness: poor vision = risky ultimate usage

hero.hero_overview:
She's the type of hero underutilized in rank games as people simply don't know how she works.

She's mainly used as either a turret defender or to secure a turret. Her ult disincentivizes the enemy from approaching too near her range due to how painful <Feathered Airstrike> is when she's snowballing. Another benefit of her ult is that she's not limited by what's in front of her, and can target the backline's ADC in order to zone them away and force them to recall. You can also do some spicy combos such as Hadji + Oheb's insane Feathered Airstrike + Renner Shot to knock off a squishy out of the Land of Dawn, or Novaria plus Pharsa.

The main problem of solo queueing or rank games is the same problem as to why Brody and Beatrix aren't that popular:

Most players don't know that the main purpose of these heroes is to maximize pressure and push lanes with the help of a team instead of winning 1 v 1 fights and disorganized skirmishes. She's a monster with 5 man setups for the same reason.

The only problem with Pharsa in pro play is simply that she's too immobile and prone, she doesn't have spam slow, lacking the utility needed for the current meta, as well as lacking sustained damage in a short amount of time (thanks to a plethora of nerfs) to be useful in an objective contests.

Oh, and ROSE GOLD METEOR killed her off.

Compare her with Lylia (who can shred and peel Tank Junglers), or Kadita (instant burst), or Valentina (copy CC), or Gord (ultra slow as long as you ban out Rafaela), and she comes out as lacking.

Especially in a meta where Claude/Irithel picks with Angela riding them are emphasized.
hero.hero_strength: Pharsa is a strong character with a magic-based skill set that allows her to deal damage and stun enemies
hero.hero_weakness: Valentina, a top-tier mage hero, poses a formidable threat to Pharsa, making her game significantly more challenging. Valentina's arsenal includes high mobility and a unique ability to mimic the ultimate skill of her enemies, granting her a versatile advantage in combat.

When Pharsa does her ultimate or even becomes a bird, Franko's expert hook will always be able to thwart Pharsa's actions.

spell.spell_desc: Can shoot enemy from far

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Always maximize Pharsa's ability to burst. Despite of not being meta heroe, she's still good for a pick and useful in teamfights specially her ultimate (feathered air strike).

counters.counter_desc: Always maximize Pharsa's ability to burst. Despite of not being meta heroe, she's still good for a pick and useful in teamfights specially her ultimate (feathered air strike).

dominants.dominant_desc: My highest ranking on Pharsa is Global No.4 and if there's  one thing I noticed to her is the positioning. Pharsa can be easily take down by burst heroes as well, so it is a must to maximize the range of your ultimate. Prior to that is the rotation, after clearing up your lane, you can immediately rotate to other lanes  .
dominants.dominant_title: Effective during lvl. 4-5, and good for teamfights.

emblems.emblem_desc: For my emblem set, I use Rapture as primary, for extra adaptive penetration.  For my secondary, I prefer wilderness blessing, this will allows you to rotate faster. Lastly, Impure range for extra mana and damage/penetration.

equips.equip_desc: I often use this set of items for bursting heroes specially if the enemy's pick is more on sustain.This items contributes for a bigger magic attribute and penetration which I think is useful.

Ever since Sky Piercer was released, I started building it together with glowing wand which has extra burn effect. the combination  of this two items has high attribute that if effective for bursting heroes.

hero.hero_overview: Pharsa is one of the best mages in Mobile Legends. She's  often used in MPL tournament because of her ultimate's range.
hero.hero_strength: Even though Pharsa is not meta in the past few season, her ability to burst  is quite good.
hero.hero_weakness: Pharsa can be easily take down by heroes with long ranges (specially marksman). And so positioning is crucial.

spell.spell_desc: The use of spell always depends  on the enemy line-up. You can use flicker, flameshot, and purify. This three spells are useful for Pharsa, but I often use flicker

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Good taunt and provides perfect synergy when he uses his ultimate, finishing it off with Pharsa's ultimate.
The perfect synergy when he uses his ultimate finish it up with her ultimate

counters.counter_desc: When Aurora gets close to you, she can CC and burst you down. Make sure to keep your distance.
She can easily escape your ultimate by ulting and using her first skill make sure to keep your distance to avoid getting burst down.
She can stun you from afar and stop you from ulting. Make sure to keep an eye out for her incoming stuns.
Valir is vulnerable to burst damage heroes.
Pharsa can ult her from afar don't let her get close.

dominants.dominant_desc: In the early game, Pharsa is one of the few mages who can quickly burst down enemies even without any items. Make sure to take advantage of this by camping and targeting squishy heroes, while also rotating to other lanes to secure kills.
dominants.dominant_title: Before level four or level two prioritize leveling her first kill up to reduce its cool down, then follow up with her second skill to increase its damage

emblems.emblem_desc: This emblem is suitable for all mages provides high power, good penetration, and decent cool down.

equips.equip_desc: Provides high pure damage to take down non-squishy tank heroes

hero.hero_overview: Long rage, high damage mobility mage
hero.hero_strength: Long-range, high early-game damage with decent crowd control using the first and second skill combo.
hero.hero_weakness: Her ultimate ability is difficult to aim accurately due to a long delay and she is weak against assassins and other bursty heroes positioning is key during Ultimate.

spell.spell_desc: Used for escape and offense to catch the enemy off guard.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Gatotkaca can be a great ally, especially when he uses his ultimate to stun enemies. Pharsa can then follow up with her ultimate to finish them off. Even without his ultimate, his stun is also helpful in her favor.
Atlas can group enemies together with his ultimate ability, while Pharsa can serve as the finisher.
His absolute taunt can truly make her feel powerful, especially when combined with her ultimate ability.

counters.counter_desc: Benedetta moves quickly, making it hard for Pharsa to land her hits.
Selena can stun Pharsa from a distance, preventing her from using her ultimate ability.

She can cancel her ultimate during the laning phase. In team battles, positioning is key to catch her off guard due to her mediocre skill range. Camping works, but beware of her passive death immunity.
Pharsa can burst her down from a distance; she must get close to Pharsa to burst her down.
Pharsa has a longer range than Odette when she is in her ultimate state.

dominants.dominant_desc: In the early game, positioning during team fights is crucial. Always keep your distance from enemies when in your ultimate state. This strategy also applies in the late game. Especially when you don't her first skill mark on them to stun and land your blows.
dominants.dominant_title: Before leveling up to level two, prioritize upgrading the second skill, then continue to improve the first skill to reduce its cooldown. Aim to balance these two skills with their cooldowns in mind.

emblems.emblem_desc: This emblem is suitable for most mages, providing high magic power, good penetration, and a nice reduction in cooldown.

equips.equip_desc: I chose these types of equipment because it is quite effective. Pharsa has a hard time eliminating heroes when using her ultimate, as opponents often get out of it. Additionally, she can combine her first and second skills to take down squishy, non-tank enemies who lack magic defense in the late game when she has full items. Even without her ultimate, she remains one of the few mages capable of dishing out formidable damage with her skills.

hero.hero_overview: Pharsa is a high-burst mobility mage who can quickly eliminate enemies from a safe distance, with decent crowd control using her first and second skill combo.
hero.hero_strength: Pharsa is very strong in the early game. She can burst down any non-tank hero at level 4 and eliminate them if all her hits connect. When attacking an enemy, make sure to use your first skill followed by your second skill to stun them. After that, finish off with your burst damage. This strategy should typically eliminate non-tank heroes.
hero.hero_weakness:
Pharsa is vulnerable to assassins, so it's essential to position yourself wisely in team battles. Be cautious of heroes who can quickly burst you down. Always check the enemy's builds to identify who has magic defenses. Target opponents without magic defenses by attacking from a safe distance or ambushing them to burst them down

.

spell.spell_desc: Flicker is a great option for Pharsa because it allows her to escape when her fourth skill is on cooldown. Additionally, while in her ultimate state, she can use Flicker to dash forward and burst down enemies that are out of her range.
